CASE DISMISSED

Tldr:
Officer R. Torres pulled me over and ticketed me for violations I did not violate. Silveira Law came through and got my case dismissed

I was pulled over by Officer R. Torres on my motorcycle for lane splitting and speeding over the Bay Bridge when I was going the speed limit, and lane splitting is legal in CA.

Officer Torres tried to say I was going 80 in a 50. Interesting because no one drives the speed limit on Bay Bridge. Flow of traffic is 60-80. But I was about to exit so I was actually going 50mph heading to my exit, but because my motorcycle at the time was loud I guess officer Torres ignorantly assumed I was speeding. Thankfully I had a camera on my motorcycle recording everything, so I told him I was going speed limit and I have it on camera, and he decided not to charge me for speeding 80 in a 50.

He ended up charging me for

A) 22350 VC: unsafe speed while lane splitting
B) 21750(A) VC: modified exhaust
C) 16028(A) VC: no insurance

A) He was blatantly lying about my unsafe speed while lane splitting. I was going speed limit, and Officer Torres was also ahead of me until I got onto the exit ramp, so he has no idea what speed I was going nor did he see me lane splitting. He didn't even see me until I was on the exit ramp going 30mph, but what can I do against a cop's word, even if he's a liar.

B) I did have an illegally modified track exhaust so Torres was right to ticket me for that

C) I was fully insured at the time when he pulled me over. Unfortunately he said he was in a hurry and didn't want to wait for me to pull my insurance ID card on my phone so he gave me a 16028(a) VC violation and marked it non correctable. Even though I had insurance. He marked it non correctable on the ticket. Even if I didn't have insurance at the time it should be a correctable violation. So I would have had to pay close to $1,000 for this violations despite the fact I was fully insured.

I believe Officer R. Torres maybe was having a bad day and was deliberately trying to make my life difficult, because he was blatantly lying and charging me for violations I did not violate.

Thankfully Silveira law was able to pick up this case and help defend me in the court of law, and after a little over a year Silveira and the team were able to get my case dismissed with no penalties nor fines.

Thank you Silveira and team! This is the 2nd case I brought to them on my motorcycle that they got dismissed. I would give 10 stars if possible. If you're looking for a traffic lawyer, look no further. You've found the best.

Arnold has been practicing Maritime Law for several decades and that experience resulted in crafting a solid, logical and presentable case utilizing several accomplished experts to independently verify all the pertinent aspects of the matter. He is widely respected by the professionals in his field and recognized for his pioneering legal mind.

When the pandemic struck in 2020, Arnold was able to adjust and navigate the matter to a successful conclusion despite the numerous challenges that arose due to remote work transitions, reduction of staffing, disruptions to my treatment, etc.

Mr. Burns listened carefully, grasped my issues quickly, and gave me very clear, concrete advice that will help my family get through a very difficult period. I recommend him to anyone seeking a trustworthy, capable bankruptcy lawyer.

We live in an overwhelming world in an overwhelming city and while we think nothing of talking about the most intimate of subjects with people we barely know... the ugly subject of money is never discussed even with our closest friends and family...while every day we see more concern for just how much longer can San Francisco renters continue to live beyond their means ($8000 for a 3 bedroom at Market and Dolores!?!?!?!?!?!) and yet we still find ways to buy things we don't need with money we don't have to impress people we don't know.  ENOUGH.  I called Tom Burns when I was at my most overwhelmed.  Professional, smart, responsive and kind with a staff I would describe as the same, if you're needing some help on that subject you can't discuss with anyone else, don't hesitate to call Tom.  You will feel better and more in control after the first hello.  Stress kills...it might save your life.

Tom Burns is a wonderful Bankruptcy Attorney, if i could give a score higher than Excellent I would. I actually originally went with Tom over the other attorney in his suite simply bec he was less expensive, but it turned out to be the best decision I couldve made.

At a time when i was really in financial trouble and was about to get a large wage garnishment, Tom was wonderful, efficient, and best of all totally honest!
2 times during my Ch 13 Bankruptcy case he saved me from getting my Chapter 13 dismissed by the trustee. He just is one of those attorneys you are so glad you have on your side.

Im writing this right now with another potential issue with my case, but regardless of how things turn out, i know that he will 1. Tell me the truth 2. Give me the best advice possible 3. Do his best to help...

If you have a potential bankruptcy you would be wise to call Tom Burns.
